title: Add option to `skopeo copy from-img oci-archive:destination.tar` to skip some layers in the destination
body:
I create two images, one is called A:
```
FROM scratch
…
```
the other is called B:
```
FROM A
…
```
To transport the image B on another system I call
```
skopeo copy containers-storage:localhost/ABC:123 oci-archive:ABC-123.tar
```
and on the other system I call
```
skopeo copy oci-archive:ABC-123.tar containers-storage:localhost/ABC:123
```
This works as expected.

As next I rebuild only image B, keeping A unmodified.  Image A is present on the destination system as blob, not displayed by `podman images`.  Thus I want to copy only the last layer of image B.  The above copy-to-oci-archive copies all layers.

`skopeo copy` has to means to copy to an oci-archive-tar file only specific images (blobs, layers) from an image.

comments.body:
Yes; the OCI archive format does not have a concept of partial images. And `skopeo` is a tool to expose existing image copying operations, it’s not an _editor_ for image layers.

My very strong recommendation is to use a registry that contains the full set of layers; even if it is single-purpose and run in a short-term container. That would naturally avoid redundant uploads, and redundant downloads — but not decrease the total storage space necessary.

… and a registry also notably allows multi-threaded copies, with multi-threaded compression and O(1) access to individual layers; whereas `oci-archive` is structurally a linear file that must be copied as a whole, compressed as a whole, and decompressed as a whole.
For trivial setups/workflows/needs registries are overengineering.

Well, the engineering to avoid duplicates and parallelize and the like exists for registries. `podman run registry:2 -p … -v /some/temporary/directory/to/copy` might not be _that_ much of a hurdle.

The “trivial” format of `oci-archive` is not over-engineered that way, and therefore can’t do things like that. (Besides the thing with `tar` making some of the over-engineering fundamentally impossible or at least unprofitable.)
This ticket is not about advantages or disadvantages of registries.

The very first response was that the OCI image format does not allow partial images, so _exactly_ what you ask is impossible.

Skopeo could, plausibly, generate files in some non-standard extension / new file format to allow that feature; but we already have that feature, _and more_, some of which is fundamentally impossible to do with `oci-archive:` by using (possibly very temporary) registries.

So we are very unlikely to work on adding that non-standard extension / new file format.
